OBJ-NEW-1.evidence: M6 explicitly states 'derive pull_exit_phase using rebase-state-directory presence as the primary discriminant on non-zero exit, and dispatch pull-analyst only for pull_exit_phase=success (with HEAD change) or pull_exit_phase=stash_restoration_failed — never merely because HEAD changed'. AC7 has four explicit branches: (1) exit 0 -> success; (2) exit non-0 AND .git/rebase-merge OR .git/rebase-apply exists -> rebase_conflict, do NOT dispatch; (3) exit non-0 AND no rebase state dir AND HEAD changed -> stash_restoration_failed, dispatch; (4) exit non-0 AND no rebase state dir AND HEAD unchanged -> rebase_conflict conservative, do NOT dispatch. Architecture Design Step 4 matches. Technical Hints line 388 confirms rebase-state-directory as primary discriminant. Context JSON pull_analyst_design.pull_exit_phase_discriminant is consistent. All four branches are internally consistent across M6, AC7, Architecture Design, and Technical Hints.

OBJ-NEW-2.evidence: Technical Hints now explicitly distinguishes: 'the changelog-analyst role entry is the template for push-analyst and merge-analyst (allowed_tools: [Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Write]; same denied_write_path_prefixes; allowed_write_path_prefixes scoped to /tmp/agentic-commit/<kind>/). pull-analyst differs: allowed_tools: [Read, Glob, Grep, Bash] only — Write tool excluded to enforce advisory-only contract (no grant file written). Do NOT apply the changelog-analyst Write-tool template to pull-analyst.' M10, AC11, and context JSON policy_registration section all consistently specify pull-analyst as Write-excluded.

OBJ-NEW-3.evidence: S3 now reads: 'audit log entry on each push-analyst and merge-analyst dispatch and verdict (written under /tmp/agentic-commit/<role>/audit/ — covered by their role-specific /tmp/agentic-commit/<role>/* write prefix, no additional path permissions required); pull-analyst writes no audit file — its advisory report is displayed to stdout only and requires no Write tool'. The ~/.claude/logs/ path is absent from M1, M2, and S3. The /tmp/agentic-commit/<role>/audit/ path falls within the /tmp/agentic-commit/<role>/* allowed_write_path_prefix already declared in M10. No additional policy permissions are needed. The internal contradiction is resolved.

OBJ-NEW-4.evidence: Technical Hints line 388 now reads: 'pass pull_exit_phase as the enum string (not a boolean) to the pull-analyst dispatch context.' The stash_restoration_failure: true boolean is gone. The enum string 'stash_restoration_failed' is used consistently throughout Technical Hints, Architecture Design Step 4, AC7, context JSON pull_analyst_design, and M6.

evidence_quality_detail: All four previous issues now have consistent evidence chains across M-requirements, ACs, Architecture Design sections, Technical Hints, and context JSON. The rebase-state-directory discriminant is supported by reading actual pull.sh source: line 120 (git pull --rebase), line 151 (exit 1 on rebase conflict, before stash pop), line 154 (success message), line 174 (stash pop failure exit). Line numbers cited in spec are accurate against the actual pull.sh source verified in this review.
scope_alignment_detail: User requirement: '给我方案' (give me a plan). BA correctly scoped this as a design-plan-only cycle (R2). The three analyst agents, three command body modifications, policy registration, and CP_AGENTS addition all address the verbatim complaint that push/merge/pull lack agentic judgment like /commit has. No scope drift detected. MoSCoW classification is unchanged and appropriate.
investigation_completeness_detail: BA completed all required investigation: pull.sh source read for line-number accuracy (lines 120, 151, 154, 158-174 all confirmed); push.sh:38-42 fork-prefer-origin logic confirmed; policy_registry.py denial mechanism for unknown roles confirmed (lines 72-81, 269-270); changelog-analyst entry format confirmed (lines 792-818); CP_AGENTS set location confirmed (pretool-cp-checkin.py:60-72). No investigation gaps remain.
affected_file_accuracy_detail: All file paths verified to exist. Minor residual from iteration 2 (context JSON component_chain entry for commands/pull.md says line_range: 1-46 while wc-l confirmed 45 lines) remains but is cosmetic and does not affect implementation. Spec body correctly says lines 1-45.
spec_text_vs_execution_drift_detail: No internal contradictions remain. M6, AC7, Architecture Design, Technical Hints, and context JSON all use the same pull_exit_phase enum values and the same rebase-state-directory discriminant logic. push-analyst/merge-analyst Write-tool distinction is consistent across M10, AC11, and Technical Hints. S3 audit path is consistent with M10 write prefixes.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 6 issues and 1 observation: (1) ISSUE: .git/rebase-merge/.git/rebase-apply path checks are not worktree-safe — should use git rev-parse --git-path; (2) ISSUE: push-analyst remote range may disagree with push.sh @{u} no-op check in fork workflows; (3) ISSUE: AC3 does not explicitly require validating remote_url against grant; (4) ISSUE: M9/AC9 DO NOT list is incomplete (misses git checkout, switch, restore, stash, clean, etc.); (5) ISSUE: AC3/AC5 do not explicitly enumerate session_id and expiry validation; (6) ISSUE: first-push all-commits scan may over-scan long-lived branches; (7) OBSERVATION_ONLY: stash warning wording could be improved.

findings_classification.title: Rebase-state-directory checks not worktree-safe
findings_classification.codex_proposed_fix: Replace .git/rebase-merge/.git/rebase-apply with git rev-parse --git-path rebase-merge / rebase-apply
findings_classification.rationale: Valid technical concern for worktree environments. However: (a) pull.sh itself operates on a regular clone (not a linked worktree) in the dot-claude project context; (b) this is an implementation detail the dev agent can handle at coding time without a spec amendment; (c) not blocking the design plan.

findings_classification.title: Push analyst remote range vs push.sh @{u} no-op check mismatch
findings_classification.codex_proposed_fix: Either modify push.sh to align, or mark analyst as advisory-only for remote divergence
findings_classification.rationale: W1 explicitly prohibits push.sh modification (architect constraint). Analyst approving commits that push.sh then skips is not a safety failure. This is scope expansion beyond user requirement. Logged as adjacent observation.

findings_classification.title: AC3 does not require remote_url validation
findings_classification.codex_proposed_fix: Update M7, AC3, Step 4 to validate current remote_url against grant.remote_url
findings_classification.rationale: Architecture Design Step 4 already validates 'remote matches'; AC3 text is abbreviated but sufficient given Step 4 exists. Minor clarity improvement, not blocking.

findings_classification.title: M9/AC9 DO NOT list incomplete — denylist misses checkout, restore, stash, etc.
findings_classification.codex_proposed_fix: Expand to allowlist-style: only read-only git commands permitted
findings_classification.rationale: Valid structural observation. Dev agent writing the actual agent files can apply a more exhaustive allowlist or expanded denylist without a spec amendment. Consistent with existing changelog-analyst.md denylist convention.

findings_classification.title: AC3/AC5 do not enumerate session_id and expiry validation
findings_classification.codex_proposed_fix: Update AC3 and AC5 to require session_id, nonce, expires_at, repo-hash validation
findings_classification.rationale: M7 is explicit on these properties; Architecture Design Step 4 has the full validation checklist. AC3 is abbreviated but dev reading both will implement correctly. Minor text improvement.

findings_classification.title: First-push all-commits scan may over-scan long-lived branches
findings_classification.codex_proposed_fix: Use merge-base with remote HEAD/default branch for first-push base; fall back to all commits
findings_classification.rationale: Performance optimization beyond scope of user requirement. Using all commits is conservative (not wrong). Implementation agent may optimize without spec change.

findings_classification.title: Stash warning wording: partial application mention
findings_classification.rationale: OBSERVATION_ONLY per Codex. Warning is scoped correctly to stash_restoration_failed — rebase completed before stash pop, so no partial rebase application exists.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 7 Codex findings classified. 0 blocking findings. 4 minor/advisory items surfaced as out-of-scope observations. 2 out-of-scope items logged as adjacent observations. 1 nitpick rejected. Draft verdict PASS confirmed.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex finding #1: rebase-state-directory checks (.git/rebase-merge, .git/rebase-apply) are not worktree-safe in linked-worktree setups. Implementation should use git rev-parse --git-path rebase-merge and git rev-parse --git-path rebase-apply. Dev agent can apply without spec amendment.
out_of_scope_observations.recommended_action: dev agent handles at implementation time

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex finding #2: push.sh uses @{u}..HEAD for its own no-op detection while analyst uses refs/remotes/<resolved_remote>/<branch>..HEAD. In fork workflows, analyst may approve commits to fork while push.sh exits no-op because @{u} tracks origin. Not a safety failure (no harmful commits pushed), but a semantic gap. Would require relaxing W1 (no push.sh modification) to fix — separate cycle decision.
out_of_scope_observations.recommended_action: separate cycle — requires architect decision on W1 relaxation

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex finding #3: AC3 validation list names nonce, branch, head_sha, remote_name but does not explicitly name remote_url, session_id, or expires_at. Architecture Design Step 4 has the full list. AC3 text could be more explicit. Minor clarity improvement for BA to consider in a future iteration.

Codex finding #4: M9/AC9 use a denylist for forbidden git operations; an allowlist would be more complete. Implementation agent should expand the DO NOT section to include git checkout, switch, restore, stash, clean, worktree, tag, update-ref, config, remote set-url when writing the actual agent files.
out_of_scope_observations.recommended_action: dev agent applies allowlist pattern in agent file DO NOT sections

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex finding #6: first-push analysis using all commits in git log HEAD may over-scan long-lived branches. Consider using merge-base with remote HEAD/default branch when available. Out of scope for this design plan cycle; implementation agent may optimize.
out_of_scope_observations.recommended_action: implementation optimization — not required for correctness

summary: BA spec iteration 3 PASS: all 4 prior objections (OBJ-NEW-1 through OBJ-NEW-4) are fully resolved. OBJ-NEW-1 (pull_exit_phase discriminant) is correct — rebase-state-directory presence is the primary discriminant in M6, AC7, Architecture Design, Technical Hints, and context JSON consistently. OBJ-NEW-2 (Technical Hints allowed_tools) is resolved — pull-analyst Write exclusion is now explicit with a 'Do NOT apply' warning. OBJ-NEW-3 (audit log path) is resolved — S3 routes audit to /tmp/agentic-commit/<role>/audit/ which is within the existing write prefix; ~/.claude/logs/ is gone. OBJ-NEW-4 (stale boolean) is resolved — pull_exit_phase enum string is used throughout. Codex adversarial review found 0 blocking issues; 5 minor/advisory items surfaced as out-of-scope observations for dev agent or future cycles. Spec is ready for implementation.
